Tunarean Signet Ring  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FINGER
AC: 2
CHA: +10 WIS: +10 HP: +15
SV FIRE: +3 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +3 SV MAGIC: +3 SV POISON: +5
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: RNG DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Tunarean Signet Ring
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FINGER
AC: 2
CHA: +10 WIS: +10 HP: +15
SV FIRE: +3 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +3 SV MAGIC: +3 SV POISON: +5
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: RNG DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Jewelry
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Sol Invictus
Lucy Entry By:Siul - Fennin Ro
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-10 07:21:03
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Friend of the Tunarean Court

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Wakening Land


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
The real reason to get this
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2006 at 6:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592345837940
27 posts
Hey everyone.

I am currently doing this quest and I have absolutely no need for item. It is trash.

I am doing it because I am a Ranger of Tunare and, for those of you who have forgotten, this is a Role Playing game. I have my epic. Entry into the court of my goddess is a natural next step.

If I want uber lewtz I'll head to WoS, do a DoD mission or just play WoW instead.

I'm tired of the number chasing game, the "let's go to MPG instead because the lewt is better" nonsense.

This is exactly the kind of content I want now. The story, the lore and the RP aspect. Frankly, shove your Tipt/Vxed flag nonsense where the sun doesn't shine, this is good fantasy RP and I love it.
▲ Page top
The real reason to get this
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2013 at 1:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1711930561698
9 posts
Yes, true words !!!
And that's the reason , why i always return to this game .
THE CONTENT IS IMPORTANT .and nothing else .....
▲ Page top
The real reason to get this
QuoteReply
# May 13 2009 at 12:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785438283
69 posts
I've come to the same point, though I enjoy both modes of playing. I find myself drawn more to the quest and role playing than just the grinding out of XP.

Personally I think what SoE has done with the defiant gear and new zone drops and such has really trashed the old content. It is sad, so much content now goes unused. I'm not sure why they thought they had to unbalance things so much.
▲ Page top
The real reason to get this
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2007 at 6:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592772917456
153 posts
Now there's inspiration: a true roleplayer, who doesn't care about being the best -- or at least better than the majority of their fellow players -- but is only concerned with enjoying the roleplaying game they pay for. It's players like this that make playing worthwhile :)

Edited, Jul 24th 2007 9:14pm by SilverDryad

▲ Page top
ring
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2004 at 1:26 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592264744478
19 posts
yada yada yada.. quest takes way too long for the stats..the wisdom is nice but much better pieces out there if your just looking for wisdom. If its for 50+, it better have FT or haste or at least some kind of effect for the time and effort you put into.
▲ Page top
My 2cp
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2004 at 2:31 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592258463417
3 posts
Maybe this ring does suck compared to what a person can get today at higher levels. But maybe for someone just starting out it's a fun thing to do and an upgrade. Sure, I like having the high end stuff too, but alot of the fun is the accomplishment to completeing the quest and having fun with friends. After all, the game is called "Everquest". There is a alot more to this game than just "racing to have the uberest char" on the server. For some people thats fine, but for me there is alot more to this game than that. And it gives good faction. Who knows what kinda stuff you will be able to quest for later and that kind of good faction is needed for it.....
▲ Page top
Once again... why bother??
QuoteReply
# May 27 2004 at 11:25 AM Rating: Default
Sannto
View User Forum
37 posts
Why bother to go thru ALL that work? (unless your not a druid, sham or cleric). If you going to do that long quest, why not just go to GE and take down the mini-named ones and loot this https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/item.html?item=23044 It's pretty common. I've looted one for my druid, my sham and my cleic. Seems much easier than doing the quest and it's an overall much better ring.
____________________________
~Another (RETURNED) EQ Junkie - Top 5 Tunare Server~

RE: Once again... why bother??
QuoteReply
# May 27 2004 at 9:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251661327
72 posts
Uhm... this ring has 10 WIS ... twice as much WIS as the one you link to. I'm a tradeskill junkie, so the extra 5 WIS is more advantageous to me than the extra mana. Also, I enjoy doing quests.

Both rings are upgrades for me... the adventage to the 5 WIS ring being that it appears to be dropable.

#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 20 2004 at 3:58 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Nice Magelo profile! Too bad it's not real. What a loser
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 10 2004 at 12:40 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ya, and he knows it....just looking for someone to recognize him...he's the type who's been playing since '01 and has a 32 warrior to show...wonder how long it took him to look up the best gear for each slot on magelo? lol, he prlly could have leveled 4 to 5 times. diagnoses: the type who could just never figure out the game
Other reason
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2004 at 6:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775879427
20 posts
There is one other reason to wear this ring: Charm soloing. From what I gather a higher charisma helps with keeping things charmed, and +10 cha in an item that also has wis and resists is a nice addition. (Plus, I have aquired almost everything for this without realizing it) All I need to do is convince my guild to try a PoM raid and I will be set.
▲ Page top
RE: Other reason
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2004 at 10:25 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1623025200347
Scholar
218 posts
Myself (61 Druid), 59 Beastlord, 61 Shaman, 57 Pally, and 56 (?) Shaman were grouping in Plane of Mischief and we saw the Named Unicorn up. We decided to give it a go for fun.

He does cast Fear and Blind frequently, but other than that he was a pretty easy kill considering he conned RED to me. He has very high magic resists. We could not slow him and my magic line of dots would not land. Cold based nukes and disease/poison dots worked great.

As far as Charisma affecting charm, it has no bearing on the Druid line of charm spells.

Good luck on your ring quests!

▲ Page top
RE: Other reason
QuoteReply
# May 27 2004 at 8:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591798672737
21 posts
I know you posted a while ago, but your post is 100% wrong. Druid mind spells are affected just as much by Charisma as enchanted ones. This include both Harmony and Charm.

Edited, Thu May 27 21:13:02 2004

im not dong it fir the ring i mdoing it because it means some thing and i love doing quest and get to go to places i thought i never. so to me its worth the trouble.

This ring is now crap
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2003 at 7:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591759155845
20 posts
Glittering Ring of Judgement is was better. The troble you have to go through to get this ring is ridiculous. I remember when this quest was first posted. At that time the ring was kickass, top of the line stuff. I wanted on myself, but now you can camp PoJ and get a GRoJ made. They(SOE) should make this ring better.
▲ Page top
RE: This ring is now crap
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2003 at 1:21 PM Rating: Default
Spiffe
Scholar
106 posts
This ring is indeed pretty lousy now, but not because of the GRoJ. At least for druids, compare instead to Circled Toe Bones, Coral Ring, or the new Ring of Dark Intellect out of Veksar. Any of those have much better +HP and +mana, which are more important for a 50+ druid, coupled with better resists. Furthermore, they can be all bought for 2-5k instead of going through this ridiculous quest.
____________________________
Help keep Allakhazam useful -- rate posts.
▲ Page top
RE: This ring is now crap
QuoteReply
# Apr 28 2003 at 7:50 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
I can't help but disagree. By the time you can do this quest, most druids don't NEED 20 wis in one slot. Note that the GRoJ has NO SAVES, none, zip, nada.

+19 to total saves. I'd take this ring.

Mind you, I'm a resists nut, but I love being able to port into CS to quad, and resist the AOE of the cobalt drake thats right on top of me when I port in. Also, I have 255 wis without buffs, so when I have KEI, I can peel off a few pieces of my wis gear, and toss on some resist gear. With my own buffs, and KEI/resist gear setup, I have 190+ MR, and over 150 to all ther resists. If I got this ring, that would be one less peice I needed to swap when I had KEI on.

Edited, Fri May 2 20:37:14 2003
▲ Page top
RE: This ring is now crap
QuoteReply
# Jun 12 2003 at 3:04 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759409730
View User Forum
174 posts
put on glittering ring then you can switch off a couple pieces of gear that have no resists but some wis, and in the end you end up with more resists
▲ Page top
RE: This ring is now crap
QuoteReply
# May 27 2003 at 12:13 PM Rating: Default
-REDACTED-
Scholar
30 posts
Look up Band of the Visionary. It owns this ring in all respects, plus you don't have to get to PoM to get it (note that the zone in to PoM is at the back of North ToV; very nasty kos to all dragon types). It's even tradeable. The sleep walker is a difficult mob, though I know a few people that have done him single group.

While crap is a strong term, it's not far off. For the effort involved (innumerable side quests, going to PoM, etc.), though, this ring is totally not worth it.
▲ Page top
RE: This ring is now crap
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2003 at 12:54 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
I wasn't saying that there weren't rings better than this, just that the GRoJ isn't better.

My honest opinion is that GRoJ is WAY overpriced, and overrated.

As far as taking off "gear that has wis and no saves" I don't HAVE any gear like that.

And yes, band of the visionary is awesome, just wish it wasn't lore. My cleric has one, but I cannot find a decent upgrade to the imbued Diamond platinum ring on his other hand.

My druid pairs up coral ring and circled toe bones.

The ring from Nadox is a nice upgrade for resists ring.. ac, 55 hp/mana, 9 to all resists.

Well, I wouldn't say "crap" exactly, but it does seem like a lot of effort to get compared to some of the post Velious drops that have come out since.

▲ Page top
Lord Prismwing
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2003 at 8:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777409141
Scholar
16 posts
Anyone have any first hand interaction with Lord Prismwing? I've yet to see him once in Wakening Lands. I've looked for him near the Dragon Circle and near the Sifaye town north of the lake, but nothing. Even brought an SK with me to kill dixies, once, in the hopes that this would make him spawn.

Has anyone actually seen this guy or know what his place holders are?
▲ Page top
RE: Lord Prismwing
QuoteReply
# Apr 13 2003 at 5:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759683686
9 posts
After searching for over 3 weeks for Prismwing, someone said wait for a server update, as luck would have it, there was a patch that night.
The next morning, I logged in and ran around, finding him near the Sifayre Camp. I finally got my Crest of the Drixies. =) Good luck.
▲ Page top

▲ Page top
6th crest
QuoteReply
# Dec 10 2002 at 4:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762894940
12 posts
The 6th Crest is the Crest of the Fairie Dragons. I haven't completed this yet, but got the text for this quest off this site awhile back. The foreman is supposed to spawn in Wakening Lands.

You say, 'hail Lord Gossimerwind'

Lord Gossimerwind blinks at you a few times then speaks in a strangely musical draconic voice. 'You vish to azzizt ze count of ze Tunarean Faerie Dragons?'

You say, 'I wish to assist'

Lord Gossimerwind says 'I vish to ztop ze Kromzek Foreman from continuing ta deztroy thiz land. Iv you vish to azzizt you can zlay ze Foreman and bring me hiz helm az proof.

Slay the kromzek Foreman and loot his helmet and bring it back for the Crest of the Faery Dragons.
▲ Page top
Ranger Use
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 8:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252113133
3 posts
Yes, the AC is low, but it has at least a +3 to each resist, plus wisdom and a bit of hp..

If ya use this instead of a diamond one, you can still glean a bit of AC and such, and you don't lose THAT much resist..

Just my thoughts =)

why all the flaming
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2001 at 8:28 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593361479811
54 posts
I really don't get it when people start to flame an item just because theur char can't use it. Come On People. There are lots of items I can't use and I don't flame an item or post with that item because of it. The game is much more fun if some items can just be used by some class/race/deity combo's. And the quest is awesome, I love an involved and difficult quest, it makes the reward much better to start with.

▲ Page top
Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Aug 25 2001 at 6:58 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250524906
4 posts
This is a suck ring for rangers for the time it takes to get. Rule of thumb level is god ac is king i would wear a ac6 65hp ring any day over this and not waist my time doing this quest leave it for the druids this ring is awesome for druids and should be wearable by tunarean clerics instead of rangers. And for the guy below who said something about controling your agro well what do u wear a 6str 6 dex ring? Nice resist though and on Rallos I would wear it a traveling ring only.
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2001 at 8:10 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249904764
2 posts
Your rule of thumb isnt so good bud, your thinking like a full on tank, not a ranger. If you can play a ranger the way a ranger should be played, AC dont mean **** and level is just a bonus(only for where you can hunt), try playing a ranger as the extra arms on a tank, you shouldnt be taking hits in a fight, that is a waste of mana that should be used on the real tank, get your Sv's up and just be resistant to AoE and a ranger is DEADLY.
nuff said

45 ranger of Veeshan
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Dec 05 2002 at 8:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256007242
View User Forum
Scholar
50 posts
It's wonderful when you get to play a ranger how it "should" be played. However, when a party doesn't have a warrior in it, you have to be prepared to become the meat shield. If a party DOES have a warrior, and you're not geared up to play aggro control, then you're failing there too. A ranger is not ALL about AC/HP, nor is he ALL about Wis. A ranger is ALL about BALANCE.
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Apr 01 2002 at 1:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757435113
13 posts
Actually by lvl 60 Rangers end up doing a lot of tanking. For small groups rangers do very well as tank and as long as they keep their hp over 4500 and ac over 1200 they arent too terrible on the cleric's mana. At any rate the above post IS correct about this ring being not so good for the tanking ranger. However, it isnt a bad ring when resists, ac, and wisdom are needed. A great example would be Temple Veshan being the only ranger on a raid and having to buff every single melee there *I actually thought BPs casting CoE were lame untill I had to buff an entire raid of 53 people* At any rate, the resists, ac, and wisdom make this ring worth considering. Is it worth the hastle to get? well not to me though to others maybe.
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Mar 31 2002 at 11:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761195679
203 posts
Why do you think you have a taunt button? You're a tank, take a beating and like it, or go play a rogue =P If you think that a ranger is purely a damage dealer, you're probably making bad choices in gear (e.g. wearing stat rings over 6/65s).

I'd wear this ring on a raid, because its a good compromise between ac/hp and resists, and would love to have a larger mana pool in raid situations. It's also uber for tradeskills: wisdom to get skillups faster, and charisma to get them cheaper. :)

Actually, now that I think about it some more, the main reason for doing this quest (for a ranger) is RP of being a friend of the tunarean court. If I finished it, you can be sure I'd wear this ring all the time.

Edited, Mon Apr 1 00:00:50 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2002 at 6:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757593666
22 posts
actually in a well rounded group a ranger shouldn't take any damage other than riposte and spell damage. that's what a warrior is for. and if a ranger happens to be played on a pvp server and you don't wear saves gear... well consider yourself at your bind point when a necro or druid or any caster for that matter decides to root you and eat you alive. it's one of those balance things, of course hp and ac are great things to have and no melee class should be short on either one of them; but saves are also VERY important even on a blue server (at lvl 60 most people are doing a lot of raiding, where resists become god). personally i would suggest wearing a 6/65 and a good saves ring. probably diamond. along with a diamond mask just to pump up saves some more and then going for combination gear (thigns that have small saves but a little hp and some ac as well) in minor ac slots, and pure ac/hp in big ac slots (ie chest). just my 2cp
Athenya 42 druid
Dedlysilent 45 monk
datzme 37 warrior
missgiggly 38 chanter
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Jul 17 2002 at 1:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758302390
10 posts
Quote Squire sharra: actually in a well rounded group a ranger shouldn't take any damage other than riposte and spell damage. that's what a warrior is for.

Lets not exagerate here, if all you can do with your ranger is cast spells you should be a druid or another *spell oriented* (vs melee oriented) class. I agree that warriors are better tanks and have more hps but a ranger can definitely be #2 tank in the group, assuming the warrior is #1.

P.S. I agree with your comments about pvp though, resists are VERY important, if they root you you're pretty much dead lol.

Edited, Wed Jul 17 14:17:19 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2004 at 6:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255590588
View User Forum
12 posts
Okay, sorry - I know this thread is supposed to be about this ring, but I feel compelled to respond to this comment:

"Lets not exagerate here, if all you can do with your ranger is cast spells you should be a druid or another *spell oriented* (vs melee oriented) class. I agree that warriors are better tanks and have more hps but a ranger can definitely be #2 tank in the group, assuming the warrior is #1."

Ummm...speaking as a level 65 ranger, most of us actually shoot arrows at stuff, not cast spells. I'm full of mana most of the time because the majority of my energy is put into snaring mobs and then shooting as many arrows at them as I possibly can. Most of the time, you're doing this well out of melee range, so you don't incur any damage. In fact, if you get a good bow with a long range, you can be out of spell range, too, and still be doing awesome damage.

It's true that I can fill in for a tank if I absolutely have to, but it's obvious that's not the ranger's primary role. Yep, we have taunt - but it maxes at a skill level of 150. Yep, we have disarm - but it maxed at a skill level of 50. Yep, we can have a lot of hitpoints, but we'll never be a substitute for a paladin, warrior, or shadowknight in a group.

In Planes of Power groups, I'm definitely NOT a tank substitute. In somewhere like Nadox, well, I can make an okay tank. It all depends upon what you're fighting and who else you have in your group, really.

But over all, a ranger's best damage is going to be dealt from a distance - with arrows (not spells).

Now, on to this ring -- I know a lot of people say this is "crap", etc., but sometimes there's something to be said for actually working on and completing a quest. This game is called EverQUEST not EverLOOT-ALL-YOU-CAN-AND-DON'T-DO-ANY-QUESTS. Who can deny the fun and satisfaction derived from finishing such a complicated quest?

It's true this ring is certainly not the best thing in the game, but it's still a pretty nice piece with a lot of stats, and it takes some skill as a player, as well as a lot of dedication, to obtain it. Think of it not only as a piece of equipment, but also as a trophy to your level of dedication to the questing aspect of this game.

Edited, Thu Jun 10 19:47:24 2004
▲ Page top

Lets get something straight... a ranger isnt a warrior, yes. But a ranger IS a backup tank and can sometimes be called to do that in any situation. I play a cleric and although I dont like healing rangers, they arent as bad as rogues/casters etc. On a vindi raid a few days ago the warriors ate it at 10% and the rangers tanked him, and even though it cut down our mana fast we did keep the ranger up against a pretty hard hitting mob, even tho we did eventually wipe ( We all died when he was at 1% =\)
Tombe
58 Cleric
▲ Page top
RE: Too low AC
QuoteReply
# Apr 07 2003 at 10:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257248882
Scholar
155 posts
Rangers at high levels are NOT tanks.... Yes, we can fill the role in a pich if needed, at a much higher mana cost to the healer.. but we wear chain armor... there is no way we can compete with the Warriors, Paladins, and SKs out there.. and let me tell you, except for big named mobs, I would take a pally over a warrior for tanking... Chain stun is godly on most mobs... Even mobs that can't be stunned, the paly has many more resouces than the Warrior. Now on something like Vindy, AoW, etc, gimme the warrior for sheer HP/AC.

Im sorry, but the nature's defender IS NOT an epic, its just a really good quested weapon and the quest is NO WHERE NEAR as difficult as the fiery defender, which is better than this but less AC, but the stun DD proc and the stats make up for it

You CAN wear it if you worship Karana, there is nothing on the Screeny that says Tunare only, I would say judging from the pic ANY dru/rng can wear it /shrug You will notice on items that are religion based it will say Tunare or Karana, look on any imbued item screenys!!

#Anonymous, Posted: Aug 03 2001 at 2:44 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Bah poor little Druids?!?!? Hmpf!
